title: BOUNDARY_HANDSHAKE event payload
description: Payload for BOUNDARY_HANDSHAKE ledger events. Establishes a formal directional communication channel between two registered agents. Required for SUB_AGENT-to-SUB_AGENT handoffs. Not required for ORCHESTRATOR-involving handoffs, where parent-child registration suffices as the channel. Governance rationale: Invariant 3 (Evidence-First) — every peer-level sub-agent channel has an explicit evidence record. Invariant 4 (Reconstructability) — channel state is derived from ledger events, not external indices. Directionality decision (Sprint-2 decision a): a handshake establishes the A-to-B channel only; the reverse direction requires its own handshake event.

source_agent_id.description: Agent initiating the channel. Must be a registered ACTIVE AgentIdentity in this session.

target_agent_id.description: Agent accepting the channel. Must be a registered ACTIVE AgentIdentity in this session. Cannot equal source_agent_id.

handshake_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of channel establishment.

channel_purpose.description: Optional free-text tag describing the intended use of the channel. Audit-visible but not machine-enforced.
